question: [File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageService.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the sequence-number deduplication filter (t.getSequenceNumber() > last.getSequenceNumber()) is applied before onNext() updates last, creating a TOCTOU window in a concurrent reactive pipeline where two messages with the same sequence number both pass the filter and are both exported as incorrect duplicate records?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageService.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: incomingMessages()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger incomingMessages() when topicContext.isComplete() returns false but the subscription is about to complete, causing topicListener.listen(newFilter) to start a new listener subscription that delivers messages already past the endTime, which then bypass the takeWhile check if the outer flux has already been cancelled, resulting in incorrect records being exported?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageService.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user set endTime to a value in the past (but still > startTime to pass isValidEndTime()), causing isComplete() to immediately return true (filter.getEndTime() < startTime of TopicContext), and the takeUntilOther(pastEndTime()) to terminate the live flux before any messages are delivered, resulting in all live records being missing even if they exist in the database within the requested range?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageService.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: missingMessages()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the gap-recovery filter newFilter built with endTime=current.getConsensusTimestamp() where the retriever uses strict less-than comparison, causing the message at exactly current.getConsensusTimestamp()-1 to be included or excluded incorrectly depending on the retriever's boundary semantics, resulting in a missing or duplicated record at the gap boundary?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageService.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the log.info('Subscribing to topic: {}', filter) call that logs the full filter including subscriberId, startTime, endTime, and topicId, to infer internal state about other subscribers' sessions if logs are accessible, and then craft a filter that collides with another subscriber's session to cause incorrect record delivery?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageService.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: isComplete()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Instant.ofEpochSecond(0, filter.getEndTime()).plus(grpcProperties.getEndTimeInterval()).isBefore(Instant.now()) check in isComplete() where filter.getEndTime() is a nanosecond-precision long, and if endTimeInterval is 30 seconds, the subscription stays alive 30 seconds past endTime, causing messages committed after endTime but within the interval to be incorrectly exported?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageService.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user open a subscription with limit=0 (satisfying @Min(0)), causing hasLimit() to return false and flux.take() to never be applied, resulting in an unbounded stream that exports all messages indefinitely, potentially including messages the subscriber did not intend to receive, leading to incorrect record export?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageService.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: missingMessages()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the safety-check branch to build a gapFilter using topicContext.getFilter().toBuilder().startTime(startTime).build() without setting an endTime, causing the unthrottled retriever to return all messages from startTime to the present, including messages beyond the original subscription's endTime, resulting in incorrect out-of-range records being exported?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageService.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Flux.merge(safetyCheck, live) where both the safetyCheck and live Flux deliver the same message concurrently, and the outer sequence-number filter (t.getSequenceNumber() > last.getSequenceNumber()) only deduplicates based on the last processed message, allowing a race where both copies pass the filter before onNext() updates last, exporting duplicate records?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageService.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: incomingMessages()] Can an unprivileged external user cause incomingMessages() to be called when topicContext.getLast() returns null (no historical messages delivered), making startTime = filter.getStartTime(), and if the live listener starts from a later point than filter.getStartTime(), messages between filter.getStartTime() and the listener's actual start are permanently missing from the exported stream?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageService.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the doFinally(topicContext::finished) and doFinally(s -> subscriberCount.decrementAndGet()) ordering, where if finished() throws an exception, subscriberCount is not decremented, causing the gauge metric to permanently over-count active subscribers and masking resource exhaustion that leads to missing records for other subscribers?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageService.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: missingMessages()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a scenario where missingMessages() is called with a current message whose sequenceNumber is exactly last.getSequenceNumber() + 2 (one gap), causing numMissingMessages=1 and a retriever query with limit=1, but if the database returns 0 rows (message not yet committed), the gap message is permanently missing and current is still exported, creating an incorrect sequence in the exported records?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageService.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user set startTime to exactly DomainUtils.now() (the boundary of isValidStartTime()), causing a race where messages committed at that exact nanosecond are neither included in the historical retrieval (which may use strict less-than) nor in the live listener (which starts from startTime), resulting in missing records at the subscription boundary?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageService.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: topicExists()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the entityRepository.findById(topicId.getId()) lookup where the entity cache (entityCacheSize=50000) returns a stale entity type (e.g., an entity that was a TOPIC but was deleted and replaced with an ACCOUNT), causing topicExists() to incorrectly validate the topic and export records for the wrong entity type?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageService.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that subscriberCount is an AtomicLong with no upper bound, by opening millions of subscriptions to increment subscriberCount to Long.MAX_VALUE, causing subsequent incrementAndGet() to overflow to Long.MIN_VALUE and the Gauge metric to report a negative subscriber count, masking the actual load and leading to resource exhaustion that causes missing records?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageService.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: missingMessages()] Can an unprivileged external user cause missingMessages() to be invoked concurrently from both the safetyCheck Flux (subscribeOn boundedElastic) and the live Flux (concatMap), where both read the same topicContext.getLast() value simultaneously, compute overlapping gap ranges, and both call topicMessageRetriever.retrieve() for the same range, resulting in duplicate records being exported?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageService.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Mono.delay(Duration.ofSeconds(1L)) in the safetyCheck Flux, where if the historical retrieval takes longer than 1 second, the safety check fires before historical delivery is complete, causing missingMessages() to query with a startTime based on a stale last value, and the retrieved gap messages overlap with historical messages, exporting duplicate records?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageService.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: incomingMessages()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the newFilter built in incomingMessages() to have limit = filter.getLimit() - topicContext.getCount().get() = 0 when count equals the original limit, causing hasLimit() to return false on the newFilter (since limit=0 means no limit), and the listener to deliver all remaining messages without a limit, exporting more records than the subscriber requested?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageService.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the filter(t -> last == null || t.getSequenceNumber() > last.getSequenceNumber()) applied to the merged flux, where last is read from topicContext.getLast() (AtomicReference) without synchronization with onNext() updates, causing a stale read that allows a message with a lower sequence number to pass the filter and be exported as an incorrect record?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageService.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: missingMessages()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the concatWithValues(current) at the end of missingMessages() where current is appended after the gap-fill retrieval, but if the retrieval itself returns current (because the endTime filter is inclusive on the retriever side), current is exported twice as an incorrect duplicate record?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageService.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user set endTime to a value that causes isComplete() to return true before the historical Flux completes, causing takeUntilOther(pastEndTime()) to cancel the historical retrieval mid-stream, resulting in partially exported records where only some historical messages are delivered and the rest are missing?
